title: CSS Framework File Size Comparison 2026 | siliconbased.dev
description: Compare the exact minified and gzipped file sizes of over 30 major CSS frameworks, including Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap, Bulma, and more. Definitive 2026 data.

schemas.headline: CSS Framework File Size Comparison 2026
schemas.description: Compare the exact minified and gzipped file sizes of over 30 major CSS frameworks, including Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap, Bulma, and more.

acceptedAnswer.text: As of 2026, the compiled and minified Bootstrap CSS file is typically around 220KB to 240KB. When served over the network with Gzip compression, the transferred size drops to roughly 25KB to 30KB.
In production, Tailwind's compiler purges unused classes, making a typical production Tailwind CSS file usually less than 10KB, vastly smaller than Bootstrap's standard 230KB bundle.

mainEntity.name: What is the smallest CSS framework?

acceptedAnswer.text: Classless and minimal frameworks are the smallest. Frameworks like MVP.css, Water.css, and Milligram are typically under 5KB minified.

mainEntity.name: What is the most popular CSS framework?

acceptedAnswer.text: Bootstrap remains one of the most popular globally by legacy, but Tailwind CSS is arguably the most popular among modern web developers for new projects in 2026.

mainEntity.name: Do CSS frameworks slow down websites?

acceptedAnswer.text: They can if serving large unused bundles. Modern practices like purging unused CSS prevent frameworks from degrading performance.
